title: Pick the right lock for the context
description: Choose spinlock vs mutex vs RCU by whether the path can sleep and whether it runs in interrupt context, never sleeping while atomic.
body: Use when adding synchronization to kernel code. Do not take a mutex (which can sleep) in interrupt context or while holding a spinlock, and do not spin on a spinlock for a long critical section. Actions: use a spinlock (with irqsave if shared with an ISR) for short, non-sleeping critical sections, a mutex for paths that may sleep, and RCU for read-mostly data; keep critical sections minimal. Validation signals include a clean lockdep run and no might_sleep/scheduling-while-atomic warnings under stress. Risk boundary: sleeping in atomic context deadlocks or panics the kernel; treat a sleeping call under a spinlock as blocking and confirm with lockdep before merging.
